architecture: MVVM with SwiftUI: View -> ViewModel (ObservableObject) -> Service/Repository -> data source.
Inject dependencies via initializers (or the Environment); keep Views free of networking.
Domain models separate from API DTOs; map at the service/repository boundary.
Use async/await for concurrency; @MainActor for anything touching UI state.
conventions: Prefer value types (struct/enum); use Codable for models.
No force-unwraps or force-try in production; use guard for early return and optional binding.
Errors via throws / Result; represent view state with an enum (idle/loading/loaded/failed).
Explicit access control (private/internal/public); mark UI-updating members @MainActor.
Localize user-facing strings; do not hardcode copy.
lint: Run SwiftLint and SwiftFormat; fix warnings in changed scope.
No compiler warnings in touched files; avoid retain cycles ([weak self] in escaping closures).
Add XCTest cases for ViewModel/service logic.
templates: scaffold-data: Codable model + an async APIService (URLSession) + a Repository protocol/implementation.
scaffold-screen: SwiftUI View + ViewModel (ObservableObject with @Published state) + navigation wiring.
Follow the existing project/module organization; do not introduce a new pattern.
design-system: Colors/fonts from the Asset catalog or Color/Font extensions — never hardcode hex values.
Reuse shared SwiftUI components; support Dynamic Type and light/dark appearance.
Use SF Symbols for iconography; respect safe areas.
library-docs: Prefer first-party frameworks: SwiftUI, Swift Concurrency, URLSession, Combine (where already used).
Persistence: SwiftData or CoreData — match the repo.
If Alamofire/other third-party is present, follow its existing usage; do not add new deps casually.
